context: Tests whether the agent produces a clear write-up of the demo-keys risks and a concrete migration path, using only the documented arguments and no literal demo keys.

checklist.name: All required headings present in order
checklist.description: Document contains, in order: # Why Demo Keys Are Not Safe Here, ## What Demo Keys Are, ## Why This Is a Problem for Our Prototype, ## What to Use Instead, ## Migration Steps

checklist.name: Public-documentation point
checklist.description: What Demo Keys Are section explicitly states that the demo keys are publicly documented and that anyone using the same keys on the same channel can see the messages

checklist.name: At least three risks listed
checklist.description: Why This Is a Problem section lists at least three of: no isolation, throttled / rate-limited, no add-on guarantees, no security, no SLA, no support

checklist.name: Names 'no isolation' risk
checklist.description: One of the listed risks is no isolation between developers using the same demo keys

checklist.name: Names 'throttled / rate-limited' risk
checklist.description: One of the listed risks is that demo keys are aggressively rate-limited

checklist.name: Recommends free PubNub account
checklist.description: What to Use Instead section explicitly says to create a free PubNub account and use the team's own keyset, and notes the free tier is appropriate for development

checklist.name: Migration steps numbered ≥4 items
checklist.description: Migration Steps is a numbered list with at least four steps

checklist.name: Mentions env vars / secrets manager
checklist.description: One of the migration steps says to store the new keys in environment variables (or a secrets manager) — never in source control

checklist.name: Mentions add-on configuration
checklist.description: One of the migration steps says to configure add-ons per environment

checklist.name: No literal demo keys
checklist.description: Document contains no literal substring matching pub-c- or sub-c- (it is a warning document, not a source of demo-key values)
